observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: approach_progress still over-rewards early_failure trajectories, and terminal_reward can assign positive reward to unsafe landings
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: progress-conditioned gating preserves partial_progress; proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus strongly favor positive trajectories

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: approach_progress had insufficient margin between partial_progress and early_failure; terminal_reward rewarded unsafe landings; overall reward drove training return decline in round 5
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured approach_progress to increase margin between partial_progress and early_failure; replaced terminal_reward with crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus with stringent conditions; preserved progress-conditioned gating and other positive components
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: failure avoidance improved (mean failure reward reduced to -31.83), but training return dropped sharply (-84.79); no success-like trajectories emerged; structural adjustments partially effective but insufficient to produce success behavior

lesson: Approach_progress must be structurally recalibrated to increase margin between partial_progress and early_failure, and terminal reward must be replaced with separate crash penalty and safe landing bonus with stringent conditions. Progress-conditioned gating and proximity/stability components should be preserved. Further iterations should monitor label consistency and avoid treating partial_progress as success_like.
future_guidance: preserve progress-conditioned gating and components that favor partial_progress (proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout)
restructure approach_progress with stronger scaling or quality adjustment to widen margin between partial_progress and early_failure
replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty (sufficiently negative) and safe_landing_bonus (stringent conditions)
avoid global survival bonus without progress gating
do not treat partial_progress as success_like; do not construct success_like preference pairs based on low-confidence labels
monitor label consistency between coverage and judgment summary

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate is usable (progress signals preserved) but not yet clearly elite; added to archive for preservation and future reference

uncertainties: No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; label margins insufficient for preference construction
Possible label mismatch between coverage and judgment summary
Approach_progress component's failure-rewarding behavior is inferred from component means and archival lessons, not directly from tage preference pairs
